sevenDay.synopsis: We started the morning with patchy fog and LIFR ceilings, but all areas are now predominant VFR. A few light showers are not out of the question this afternoon and all areas will remain predominant VFR into the N1. Showers and TS will move into our northern areas tonight, reaching us early tomorrow morning as the approaching cold front moves in. Expect another round of IFR ceilings and vis to build in after midnight, lifting to VFR after 1000L. The front will stall to our south tomorrow afternoon, keeping rain chances in the area for a while. A few light showers with ISOLD IFR are possible Friday morning with increasing rain chances Friday afternoon/evening. We'll continue to see waves of precip develop along the boundary thru the weekend and into the early part of Monday. By mid-AM Monday we should be dry again and returning to predominant VFR conditions with no hazards.
sevenDay.title: 7-DAY OUTLOOK: 06 MAY - 12 MAY
